subject: Ecology
title: Adaptive Radiation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Adaptive Radiation.

hook.text: One finch species reached the Galápagos and evolved into 13 species—each with a different beak.

buildup.text: Adaptive radiation happens when a single ancestor diversifies to fill many empty niches.

discovery.text: Islands offer empty niches because few competitors arrive. Opportunity drives rapid evolution.

twist.text: Hawaii's honeycreepers radiated into over 50 species from a single finch-like ancestor.

climax.text: Adaptive radiation is evolution at full throttle—visible on islands within thousands of years.

punchline.text: One species arrives. A whole family radiates.

quiz.question: What drives adaptive radiation on islands?
